title: More Fox Variants API
description: Lightweight API to implement new fox variants.
body:
![Logo](https://github.com/AkashiiKun/MoreFoxVariantsAPI/blob/master/img/title.png?raw=true)

[![](https://media.discordapp.net/attachments/895334913560178698/1118530263329288362/wiki.png)](https://github.com/AkashiiKun/MoreFoxVariantsAPI/wiki) [![](https://media.discordapp.net/attachments/895334913560178698/915683907649822750/reportissue.png)](https://github.com/AkashiiKun/MoreFoxVariantsAPI/issues)

[![Modrinth](https://img.shields.io/modrinth/FGPy4Dsn?logo=modrinth&style=for-the-badge)](https://modrinth.com/mod/mfvapi) [![Modrinth](https://img.shields.io/modrinth/game-versions/FGPy4Dsn?logo=modrinth&style=for-the-badge)](https://modrinth.com/mod/mfvapi)

[![Downloads](http://cf.way2muchnoise.eu/full_1502909_downloads.svg?badge_style=for_the_badge)](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/mfvapi) [![Versions](http://cf.way2muchnoise.eu/versions/1502909.svg?badge_style=for_the_badge)](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/mc-mods/mfvapi)

 

# **Introduction**

*   Lightweight API that other mods can use to add new Fox Variants while keeping compatibility with each other
*   Does not any new variants on its own
*   Gives the possibility to add fox spawning on biomes using a custom tag.

 

# **Mod suggestions**

WIP

 

# **Information for everyone**

## **Summoning Command**

Summoning fox can be now done with a string value as the "variant" nbt.

This is similar to the namespace system minecraft uses almost anywhere else but doesn't for some entity variants included the fox's.

For example this would be how to summon the vanilla snow variant:

```
/summon minecraft:fox ~ ~ ~ {variant:"minecraft:snow"}
```

Or to summon a variant from custom variants:

```
/summon minecraft:fox ~ ~ ~ {variant:"modid:variant"}
```

 

# **Information for Modders**

Information dedicated to modding using the API can be found in the [Wiki](https://github.com/AkashiiKun/MoreFoxVariantsAPI/wiki).- Gives the possibility to add fox spawning on biomes using a custom tag.
